Dear Middle School Learners,

 

We are excited to announce that Community Service is back, and we're kicking off the year with one of our favourite initiatives – Our School, Our Responsibility! 🏫✨

We are looking for enthusiastic volunteers to use one of the upcoming CTT periods to visit every section from EY to Grade 8 and spread the word about this initiative.

Your role will be to inform learners that:

Every class shares the responsibility of keeping their classroom, corridors, staircases, and school surroundings clean and well-maintained.
Classes will be monitored every month based on their cleanliness and responsibility.
The best-performing classes will receive Certificates of Appreciation at the end of each month.

This is a wonderful opportunity to develop your leadership, communication, and teamwork skills while making a positive impact on our school community.

If you would like to volunteer, and be the Community service Scouts, please let me know at the earliest. We look forward to having you represent the Community Service team and inspire our younger learners.

Let's work together to make our school cleaner, more responsible, and even more beautiful!

Stay geared up—this is just the beginning! We have many more exciting Community Service projects planned for the year.

Thank you!

 

Chaitali Mukherjee

Community Service Coordinator

Hi everyone 

As we wrap up this year’s community service initiatives, we would love to hear your voice and reflections on the experiences you were part of.

Please take a few minutes to fill in the form shared below. Your responses will help us understand:

How you felt during the initiatives
What you enjoyed or found meaningful
Anything you didn’t enjoy or found challenging
Your suggestions for making these initiatives even better

Your honest feedback is very important to us and will help shape stronger and more impactful opportunities for you and future learners.

Thank you once again for your enthusiasm and participation throughout the year — it has truly made a difference 

Looking forward to reading your reflections!

Dear Team,

We are pleased to inform you that several new mentorship initiatives are being planned for the month of March for our Early Years and Primary learners.

The first initiative will focus on a Green Awareness Campaign, aimed at promoting environmental responsibility and sustainability. As part of this effort, we plan to provide saplings or seeds for students to plant within the school compound or in pots. This hands-on activity is designed to help inculcate a sense of responsibility towards protecting our planet. You will also mentor them with a small PPT presentation educating them about  this.

Another upcoming initiative will centre on Sugar Awareness, encouraging students to make healthier choices by understanding the impact of excessive sugar consumption on their well-being.

Additional initiatives are currently being planned and will be communicated in due course.

We invite Middle School volunteers to participate in supporting these meaningful activities. Kindly fill out the form shared below to indicate your interest and availability. The specific dates will be updated later, and the time for all sessions will be during CTT.

We look forward to your enthusiastic participation and support.

 

Please fill the below form for showing your interest in participation.:

Community volunteer registration form – Fill out form

Warm regards,
Chaitali Mukherjee

Steps to Log In:*

 

1. Click on the link: https://app.komodowellbeing.com/login
2. Click on Log in with  Microsoft.
3. Enter your school Microsoft ID (starting with INSMI----@sanctamaria.in) and password to access your account.
4. Once you log in, a "Start Survey" message will appear. Please complete the survey.
5. If you would like, you can answer the optional questions and then click 'Submit.'

📢 NOTICE: Novel Assessment – Of Mice and Men

Dear Students,

The Novel Assessment for Of Mice and Men will be conducted during your English lesson in the last week of August 2026. The assessment consists of two parts (20 marks).

📖 Part 1: Annotations (10 Marks)

Submit a minimum of 2 meaningful annotations per page from the novel.
Annotations may include interesting vocabulary, figurative language, literary devices, symbols, themes, questions, predictions, connections, and personal reflections.

🎨 Part 2: Creative Presentation (10 Marks)

Present your understanding of the novel through either a PowerPoint Presentation or a Chart. Include:

Brief summary
Main characters
Major themes
Timeline of key events
Favourite scene/quotation with explanation
Personal reflection

Those who are doing a PPT, please upload your PPTs in the Assignment that will be created in your respective English teams, closer to the due date.

📌 Creativity, organisation, clarity, content accuracy, and presentation will be considered during assessment.

Please begin preparing well in advance and approach your English teacher if you have any questions.

Warm Regards,
English Department

🌟 Is Your Child Ready to Represent India on the Global Stage? 🌍✈️

 

 

We are thrilled to share an extraordinary opportunity for your child .The school is officially partnering with the Indian Academic Championship (INDAC) 2026-27—the country's gateway to the world-renowned International Academic Competitions (IAC, USA). 🏆If your child loves Science, History, or Geography, this is their chance to shine globally!🔥

 

Why Should Your Child Participate?🌍
1.  Dream Destinations: Top performers can qualify to represent India at the Asian Academic Championships in Thailand or the International History Olympiad in Berlin, Germany!
1. 🆓 100% FREE First Round: The initial Online Regional Qualifying Exam (ORQE) costs absolutely nothing. Students just need to score 50% or above to move to the Regionals.🧠 
2. Fun, Buzzer-Based Formats: Features exciting individual "Bees" and collaborative "Team History Bowls."
3. 🏅 Shiny Rewards: Certificates for all participants, plus stunning medals and plaques for winners!

 

📅 Key Details At A Glance:
Who Can Join: All students from Grades 6 to 12 studying in schools across India
Competitions: History Bee, Geography Bee, Science Bee, & History Bowl.  
First Step: Online Regional Qualifying Exam (ORQE) starting July 2026.
How to Register: Participation in the first round is completely free, but registration is mandatory. Click the link below to sign your child up today:👉 https://programs.live.uniquotient.com/program/1399482 👈

 

Let’s unlock your child’s global potential together! For more details, check out www.indac.in.  

 

Warm regards,
Team INDAC
(95204 14484)

Dear Students,

We are thrilled to share an incredible opportunity for you to give back to society, learn life-saving skills, and be part of a meaningful youth-led movement. Our esteemed alumna, Ms. Aadya Agarwal, is launching a nationwide First Aid Initiative, and she is looking for passionate student volunteers to join the core team!

## About the Initiative

In emergency situations, the right help at the right time saves lives. This youth-led initiative aims to increase first aid awareness and provide accessible, hands-on learning opportunities for students and local communities across India. 

## Who Can Apply?

We are looking for enthusiastic individuals who are dependable, empathetic, and eager to learn. No prior medical or first-aid background is required!

 

## How to Apply

If you are ready to step up and make a difference, please fill out the short volunteer registration form below:

Registration Link: First Aid For All Initative - Volunteer Form

 

The Bio of the student is:

 

I am Aadya Agarwal, a Year 10 student at Solihull School. I have spent time between the UK and India, which has allowed me to gain insight into different healthcare and community needs. I have a strong interest in medicine, public health, and community service, and I have previously been involved in health education and awareness initiatives.

I started the First Aid for All initiative because I believe that basic first aid is an essential life skill that everyone should have. Through my experiences in both the UK and India, I have seen how important it is for people to know how to respond confidently in an emergency before professional medical help arrives. First aid saves lives. Research shows that immediate CPR can double or even triple a person’s chance of survival following cardiac arrest, while studies suggest that up to 59% of injury-related deaths may be preventable when appropriate first aid is provided before emergency services arrive. However, many young people do not have access to practical first aid education or feel prepared to act in such situations.

The aim of this initiative is to increase first aid awareness and provide accessible learning opportunities for students and local communities in India. We hope to empower young people with the knowledge and confidence to respond effectively in emergencies while also giving volunteers the opportunity to develop leadership, communication, and teamwork skills through meaningful community service.

 

PLEASE NOTE: IF YOU HAVE REGISTERED FOR THE INITIATIVE, KINDLY BE PRESENT FOR THE UPCOMING MEETING SESSIONS .

Grade 5 Humanities – Homework Instructions
Complete the homework neatly in your Humanities notebook.
Read each question carefully and answer in complete sentences where required.
Use colours, labels, and drawings neatly for the creative task.
Write your name, class, and date at the top of the page.
Due Date: 14 July 2026 (Tuesday)
Please submit your homework on time
Part 1- Biome Research

Choose one biome (Desert, Forest, Grassland, or Tundra) and complete the table below.

Feature 

	

Your Answer 




Name of the biome 

	

 




Climate 

	

 




Where is it found? 

	

 




Two plants found there 

	

 




Two animals found there 

	

 




How are the plants adapted to survive? 

	

 




How are the animals adapted to survive? 

	

 

 

Part 2: Critical Thinking

Answer in complete sentences.

Why can a camel survive in a desert but not in the tundra?
How does climate affect the plants and animals found in a biome?
If the climate of a biome changed, what might happen to the plants and animals living there?

 

Part 3: Creative Task

Draw one plant and one animal from your chosen biome and label two adaptations that help each survive.

 

Reflection 

If you could visit one biome, which would you choose and why?

Write 4–5 sentences explaining your choice. Include what you would like to see, learn, and experience there.

Grade 5 Humanities – Homework
Topic: Exploring the Biomes of Asia

Name: ____________________  Class: __________  Date: __________

Homework Instructions
Complete the homework neatly in your Humanities notebook.
Read each question carefully and answer in complete sentences where required.
Use colours, labels, and drawings neatly for the creative task.
Due Date: 21 July 2026 (Tuesday)
Please submit your homework on time
Part A: Think and Explore

Next class, we will learn about the four main biomes of Asia:

Tropical Rainforest
Desert
Taiga (Coniferous Forest)
Tundra

Read the names of the biomes and answer the following questions.

Which biome do you think would be the hottest? Why?
Which biome do you think would be the coldest? Why?
Which biome do you think receives the most rainfall?
Which biome would you like to learn more about?
Part B: Reflection

Imagine you are going on an adventure to one of these four biomes. Which biome would you choose and what do you think you might see there?

Write 2–3 sentences.

Challenge 

Choose one biome from the list and draw what you think it might look like. Label two things you think you would find there (for example: plants, animals, mountains, snow, or sand).

Homework Instructions
Topic: The Four Major Biomes of Asia

Instructions:

Complete this homework in your Humanities (HM) notebook.
Write the date and title: The Four Major Biomes of Asia.
Write the name of each biome as a heading.
Answer both the Critical Thinking and Think and Reflect questions for each biome.
Write your answers in complete sentences.
Use your own ideas and explain your answers clearly. You may research the topic if needed.
Write 2–3 sentences for each answer.
Due date: July 28, 2026.

Dear learners, do only 2 questions of the Biome you are working on in class

 1. Tropical Rainforest

Critical Thinking: Why do you think the tropical rainforest has so many different plants and animals?

Think and Reflect: What would you enjoy most about exploring a tropical rainforest? Why?

2. Desert

Critical Thinking: How do you think plants and animals survive with very little water in the desert?

Think and Reflect: Which desert adaptation do you think is most useful? Why?

3. Taiga (Coniferous Forest)

Critical Thinking: How do you think plants and animals survive the long, cold winters in the Taiga?

Think and Reflect: Would you like to live in the Taiga? Why or why not?

 4. Tundra

Critical Thinking: Why do you think only a few types of plants can grow in the Tundra?

Think and Reflect: What might be the most difficult part of living in the Tundra? Why?

Dear Learners,

All the PPTs we used in class and the worksheets with answer keys are updated under Classwork module. Do check them out. The softcopies of the textbook and the workbook are shared as well.

 

Path: English teams>Classwork>T-1Science Fiction/ T-1Grammar/ T-1 Practice worksheet

 

Regards,

English Teachers

As children move into Grades 5, reading becomes more than a classroom activity, it becomes an essential tool for independent learning across all subjects. Our reading plan at this stage is designed to support learners as they transition from learning to read, to reading to learn. We engage them with meaningful texts, discussions, reading logs, and shared reading experiences that develop comprehension, vocabulary, and the ability to think critically about what they read. These routines not only support academic achievement but also promote independence and a lifelong love for reading. Here is a quick look at how we are encouraging strong reading habits this year:

🔹 Wednesday Reading Homework: Every Wednesday, learners receive a reading homework task, which is posted in Microsoft Teams.

🔹 Reading Log: Learners maintain a reading log in their notebooks to record the books they read, helping them develop consistent reading

🔹 ER (English Reading) Lessons: Dedicated reading lessons are part of our English curriculum, where the whole class explores a selected book together. These sessions are designed to develop key reading skills and deepen comprehension.

🔹 DEAR (Drop Everything and Read): We will also be running the DEAR (Drop Everything and Read) programme during CTT time at school to build a strong reading culture.

🔹 Book of the Month: We highlight a special book each month for learners to enjoy, discuss, and reflect on, fostering shared reading experiences across the grade.

🔹 Recommended Reading Lists: Every term, we will post a curated list of engaging reads to suit different interests and reading levels.

🔹 Library Book Issue: Learners regularly borrow books from our school library, with the help of their teachers, giving them access to a wide range of stories to enjoy at home.

🔹 Exciting Reading Events: To bring books to life, we will be organising activities like book reviews and character dress-up days based on the recommended reading list. These events aim to make reading more fun and meaningful!

 

We know that sustained reading habits require a strong partnership between school and home. When parents encourage regular reading at home and model enthusiasm for books, children are far more likely to become motivated, self-directed readers. With your continued support, we aim to enable every learner to grow as a confident reader and an independent learner. We believe that reading not only strengthens academic success but also opens doors to imagination, empathy, and lifelong learning. Thank you for supporting your child’s reading journey at home!

Dear Learners,

Your homework for today is to read the extracts of Helen Fox's story ‘Eager’ on pages 61, 63, 66, 67, 70, 72, 74, & 78 of the Learner’s Book and answer the questions posted below on an A4 size paper. Due date:22/07/26, Wednesday.

Questions:

1. Identify and copy three jobs which the house does. (Page 61)
2. Explain what a gobetween does. (Page 61)
3. Why did it seem like Gavin was wrestling in his dream? (Page 61)
4. Why is Grumps (the butler robot) shouting? (page 67)
5. Who saved Gavin from the marauders and how? Explain in your own words (page 70)
6. What made Marcia suspicious about Boadicea? (page 74)

7. How was Marcia able to escape? (page 74)

8. Look at page 78 (first line) ‘…the light from the flying pod long since swallowed by darkness’. Which literary device is used here?

9. In your own words, describe the setting for the event on page 78.

10. Which word in the text means ‘jump’? (page 78)

11. The writer uses a number of techniques to make the scene on page 78 exciting. Can you find an example of the following?
a) Two powerful adjectives:
b) Precisely described sounds:
c) Repetition:
d) A short sentence:
Regards,

English Teachers

Dear Parents,
We will be sharing optional practice worksheets (soft copies) on the English Teams platform to support learners' practice. Please note that these are not mandatory. Students are encouraged to complete them independently and use the suggested answers (shared one or two days after) for self-assessment and reflection. 

Path: English teams>Classwork>T-1 Practice Worksheets>worksheets available
Happy Learning!

Dear Parents,

 

At the Primary Division, classroom teaching and learning will primarily involve the use of textbooks and teacher-created resources, such as worksheets, which are aligned with the Cambridge Primary Progression Papers.

However, in response to requests from parents for additional home resources, we have also introduced workbooks that students may keep at home. Teachers will assign regular homework in these workbooks, allowing students to get extra practice at home and benefit from both classroom resources and home-based resources (workbooks).

To support students, teachers will continue to clarify any doubts related to workbook tasks during class discussions. Additionally, once a unit is completed in class, the answer key will be uploaded to the Microsoft Teams Classroom so that students can self-reflect on their learning.

If a workbook is needed at school for any activity, learners will be informed in advance through classroom instructions and daily logs, so they can bring it the following day.

We request that workbooks remain at home by default to help reduce the daily weight of school bags, unless specifically requested by the teacher.

Thank you for your continued cooperation and support.

 

Best Wishes

Math Teachers Grade 5

Dear Learners,

Get ready to think, solve, and shine!

We are thrilled to announce the Contentio Mathematica – Preliminary Round for Grades 1 to 5, to be held on Monday, 27th July 2026.

This exciting mathematics quiz is designed to challenge your logical thinking, strengthen your problem-solving skills, and, most importantly, nurture your love for mathematics.

The questions will be based on all the topics covered in the previous grade level and the current grade up to 15th July 2026.

Syllabus for Contentio Grade 5 will include

Previous Grade level – (PDF's of these Units are attached below)

Unit 2 – 2D shapes and patterns
Unit 6 – 3D shapes
Unit 7 – Fractions, decimals and percentages
Unit 11 – Multiplication and division
Unit 16 – Time
Unit 17- Number and the Laws of Arithmetic

Current grade level –

Unit 1 - The Number system
Unit 2 - Numbers and Sequences
Unit 4 – Addition and Subtraction -1

What's even more exciting?

The semi-finalists and finalists from each grade will earn the opportunity to proudly represent their House in the Contentio Mathematica Grand Finale.

 

Important Dates:

 

Preliminary Round: Monday, 27th July 2026

Grand Finale: 6th August 2026

 

So, start revising, sharpen your mathematical thinking, and give it your very best.

This could be your chance to shine and bring glory to your House!

We look forward to enthusiastic participation from all our young mathematicians.

Warm regards,

The Mathematics Department

Dear learners, do only 2 questions of the Biome you are working on in class

 1. Tropical Rainforest

Critical Thinking: Why do you think the tropical rainforest has so many different plants and animals?

Think and Reflect: What would you enjoy most about exploring a tropical rainforest? Why?

2. Desert

Critical Thinking: How do you think plants and animals survive with very little water in the desert?

Think and Reflect: Which desert adaptation do you think is most useful? Why?

3. Taiga (Coniferous Forest)

Critical Thinking: How do you think plants and animals survive the long, cold winters in the Taiga?

Think and Reflect: Would you like to live in the Taiga? Why or why not?

 4. Tundra

Critical Thinking: Why do you think only a few types of plants can grow in the Tundra?

Think and Reflect: What might be the most difficult part of living in the Tundra? Why?
